region_of_origin: USA — Clif Bar & Company; available at NTUC FairPrice, Cold Storage, Lazada, Shopee in Singapore
food_subgroup: Endurance energy bar — carbohydrate-dominant formulation; rolled oat base with beta-glucan fibre; 68g bar

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI exists for a branded endurance energy bar. Closest parent concept would be 'cereal bar food product' (FOODON:03301710); not assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Cereal bars — closest FoodEx2 category for oat-based energy bar; assigned as closest_parent match
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code specific to branded endurance sports bars; A0FJP (cereal bars) used as closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for branded endurance energy bar product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Clif Bar Chocolate Chip serving is the entire 68g bar, providing approximately 250 kcal, 42g carbohydrate, 21g sugar, and 10g protein.

nutrient_source.title: Clif Bar Chocolate Chip — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry; cross-referenced with Clif Bar product label (Singapore market)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Clif Bar Chocolate Chip, confirmed against the product label available in the Singapore market (NTUC FairPrice / Cold Storage). Per-100g values derived by scaling from the 68g bar label values. All core macros and micronutrients confirmed at confidence 1.0.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of oat-based cereal bars — analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database oat entries and published literature on beta-glucan modulation of GI; Clif Bar GI ~45–50 referenced in sports nutrition literature
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate basis); peer-reviewed sports nutrition literature
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct University of Sydney GI Database entry exists for Clif Bar Chocolate Chip. GI ~47 is an analogous estimate based on: (1) rolled oats GI ~55 (University of Sydney GI Database); (2) beta-glucan soluble fibre in oats is well-documented to reduce postprandial glycaemic response by approximately 10–20%; (3) published sports nutrition literature consistently reports Clif Bar GI in the 45–50 range. Glycemic load per 68g bar = (47 × 42g carbohydrate) / 100 = 19.7. Confidence 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 150mg per bar (221mg/100g); high sugar and carbohydrate load relevant for hypertension with metabolic syndrome
hypertension.guidance: Sodium per bar (~150mg) is moderate for a snack and unlikely to be the primary concern; however, the high sugar content (21g/bar) and total carbohydrate load (42g/bar) are clinically relevant for patients with hypertension co-existing with metabolic syndrome or insulin resistance, and regular consumption should be discouraged outside of active endurance exercise contexts.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: 42g carbohydrate and 21g sugar per bar; glycemic load 19.7 per bar; carbohydrate-dominant formulation misidentified as a protein snack
type2_diabetes.guidance: T2DM patients must not use Clif Bars as protein snacks: each 68g bar delivers 42g carbohydrate and 21g sugar — equivalent to approximately one bowl of white rice — with only 10g protein; the GI ~47 is medium-low due to the oat beta-glucan base but the glycemic load of 19.7 per bar is moderate-high for a sedentary snack, and this bar should be reserved exclusively for use during or before prolonged endurance exercise.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sugar (21g/bar), moderate saturated fat (2.0g/bar), oat beta-glucan provides modest LDL-lowering benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For patients with cardiovascular disease, the high added sugar content (21g/bar) contributes to hypertriglyceridaemia risk and should be limited to active exercise contexts; the oat beta-glucan fibre (~5g/bar) provides a modest LDL-cholesterol-lowering benefit, but this does not offset the overall high sugar and carbohydrate load for sedentary CVD patients.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sugar (21g/bar) contributes to pro-inflammatory dietary pattern; soy phytoestrogen content
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: High sugar content (21g/bar) contributes to a pro-inflammatory dietary pattern in RA patients not engaged in regular exercise; the soy content (roasted soy nuts, soy rice crisps) provides phytoestrogens and RA patients on oestrogen-modulating medications should be made aware.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Clif Bar Chocolate Chip (68g) is a commercially produced endurance sports nutrition bar manufactured by Clif Bar & Company (USA) and widely available in Singapore at NTUC FairPrice, Cold Storage, Lazada, and Shopee. The bar is built on a rolled oat and organic brown rice syrup base, with soy protein isolate, roasted soy nuts, soy rice crisps, and chocolate chips. It is designed for carbohydrate fuelling during prolonged endurance exercise such as cycling, running, or hiking. Per 68g bar: approximately 250 kcal, 42g carbohydrate, 21g sugar, 10g protein, 6g fat, 5g dietary fibre, and 150mg sodium. Per 100g: 368 kcal, 61.8g carbohydrate, 30.9g sugar, 14.7g protein, 8.8g fat, 7.4g dietary fibre. The glycemic index is approximately 47 (low-medium), moderated by the oat beta-glucan soluble fibre, but the glycemic load per bar is 19.7 — moderate-high for a sedentary snack. The critical clinical message for dietitians and clinicians: Clif Bar is a CARBOHYDRATE-dominant bar, not a protein bar. Patients who consume it as a protein snack are primarily ingesting carbohydrate (42g) and sugar (21g) with only moderate protein (10g). Comparison with other Singapore bar archetypes: Quest Bar (protein-dominant: 21g protein, 14g fibre, 1g sugar), Clif Bar (carbohydrate-dominant: 10g protein, 21g sugar), Kind Bar (fat-dominant: 5g protein, 9g sugar, 14g fat). The bar is vegetarian, vegan, dairy-free, and halal-suitable. It contains soy and oats (gluten-containing grain) as allergens. The oat beta-glucan provides modest LDL-lowering and satiety benefits but does not offset the high sugar load for sedentary or T2DM patients.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Clif Bar Chocolate Chip, cross-referenced with the product label available in the Singapore market. All core macros and micronutrients confirmed at confidence 1.0. GI is an analogous estimate (confidence 0.9) based on University of Sydney GI Database oat entries and published sports nutrition literature, as no direct GI Database entry exists for this specific product.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported in the USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Clif Bar Chocolate Chip as of 2026-04-13. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for this product. trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central and product label. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central and product label (plant-based formulation). vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero per product label; no vitamin C fortification in this formulation. All other micronutrients reflect fortification values from the product label as reported in USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods. GI ~47 is an analogous estimate; no direct University of Sydney GI Database entry exists for Clif Bar Chocolate Chip. Glycemic load per 68g bar = (47 × 42) / 100 = 19.74, rounded to 19.7. This record covers the Singapore market SKU (68g bar) sold at NTUC FairPrice and Cold Storage; formulation may differ from other regional SKUs.

improvement_actions_taken: Restructured input JSON into RESULT template format.
Added mandatory multilingual local_names: Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi for Singapore market product.
Expanded aliases flat array and aliases_by_language to include all language forms; verified consistency between aliases_by_language and flat aliases array.
Added cardiovascular_disease health context condition (missing from input).
Renamed 'diabetes' condition key to 'type2_diabetes' with human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' per scoring model requirement.
Populated gi_source as structured object with title, organization, URL, accessed_date, source_match_type, and source_match_notes.
Added FoodEx2 code A0FJP (cereal bars) as closest_parent with gap note; retained null for FoodOn and SNOMED CT with honest gap notes.
Documented all -1 fields (monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, water_g) and confirmed-zero fields (trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg) in notes.
Expanded natural_language_description to exceed 200 characters with clinical, nutritional, and contextual detail.
Added classification section with preparation_method, processing_level, main_ingredients, allergens, and dietary_flags.
Added serving section with description, size_g, and natural_language_portion.
